title: Rudgea stenophylla
intro: Rudgea stenophylla is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, commonly known as "Rudgea" or "Mira de San Juan". The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rudgea stenophylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Rudgea stenophylla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and digestive disorders.
